description_long: This is a roughly rectangular stone with a striking two-tone appearance. The upper surface is covered in a dense layer of translucent, amber to golden-brown crystals, giving it a jagged and sparkling texture. The lower portion is a solid, chalky white stone with a matte, uneven finish and some subtle layering. The transition between the crystal and stone layers is irregular, with some crystals extending slightly down the sides. The stone appears natural and uncut, with no visible tool marks or artificial shaping. Its overall form suggests it could serve as a decorative or functional barrier in an urban landscape.
description: A rectangular stone with a white base and a jagged, amber crystal-covered top, featuring a rough, natural texture.
description_view_0: This view highlights the dense cluster of amber crystals on the top and the layered, chalky white stone beneath.
description_view_1: The side profile shows the gradual transition from the crystal layer to the white stone, with fewer visible crystals.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the irregular, jagged crystal surface and the rough, blocky shape of the stone.
description_view_3: The rear view displays more of the white stone base, with the crystal layer receding and the rough texture prominent.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a low barrier
attribute_pattern: Irregular crystalline pattern on the top surface, with subtle horizontal layering in the white stone base.
attribute_weathering: The stone appears rough and unpolished, with some minor surface pitting and no significant signs of erosion or lichen.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or grassy urban environments, standing out as a decorative or functional natural element.
